title: lepidium graminifolium var. suffruticosum
intro: Lepidium graminifolium var. suffruticosum, also known as field cress or creasy greens, is a type of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that grows as a perennial herb. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and a stimulant, and to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Lepidium graminifolium var. suffruticosum include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve overall health. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, and is also consumed raw in salads or as a cooked green.
